explanation: The rule specifically looks for a parent-child relationship where msiexec.exe is the parent and contains the '-Embedding' flag (used when msiexec is launched as a COM server). By using the shell concatenation operator '&' or 'start', the shell (cmd.exe) becomes the parent of the malicious process instead of msiexec.exe, even if msiexec was the initial process in the chain.

The rule filters based on the Image path ending in '\powershell.exe', '\pwsh.exe', or '\cmd.exe'. By copying the executable to a different name (e.g., not_ps.exe), the 'selection' criteria for the Image field is no longer met, even if the ParentCommandLine contains the targeted strings.

The rule contains an explicit exclusion for cmd.exe if the CommandLine contains the path to the Splunk Universal Forwarder. An attacker can craft a command line that includes this specific string as a dummy argument or file path to trigger the 'filter_splunk_ufw' logic, causing the SIEM to ignore the event.
